last_updated: 2026-03-20 (NM updated: HB 212 comprehensive law Apr 2025, fluoropolymer exemption; MN: PRISM Jul 1 2026 deadline)

MN.name: Minnesota
MN.summary: Most comprehensive state PFAS law. PRISM registration portal launched Jan 2026 (v1.11 as of Mar 2026). $800 one-time reporting fee per manufacturer. Annual updates due Feb 1 each year.
key_dates: Jul 1, 2026: PFAS manufacturer registration deadline in PRISM portal (extended from Jan 1, 2026)
Feb 1, 2027: First annual PRISM update due
Jan 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in cookware, food packaging, children's products already in effect
Jan 1, 2032: Full ban on intentionally added PFAS in all products
MN.andersen_note: HOME STATE — ACTIVE DEADLINE. Registration in PRISM portal (prism.theic2.org) required by Jul 1, 2026. $800 one-time fee. Weatherstripping/seal fluoropolymer coatings are subject to Amara's Law. Contact: pfasreporting.mpca@state.mn.us

ME.name: Maine
ME.summary: First state to pass comprehensive PFAS law. Requires disclosure of intentionally added PFAS by 2023, phase-out by 2030.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2023: PFAS disclosure to state required
Jan 1, 2030: Ban on sale of products with intentionally added PFAS
ME.andersen_note: Products sold in ME may require PFAS disclosure. Check weatherstripping and seal coatings.

WA.name: Washington
WA.summary: Comprehensive PFAS phase-out across food packaging, firefighting foam, apparel, ski wax, and more.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2023: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
Jan 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in cookware, carpets, ski wax
Jan 1, 2026: Ban on PFAS in apparel
WA.andersen_note: Products sold in WA subject to multiple category bans. Review product lines sold in this market.

CA.name: California
CA.summary: Multiple PFAS laws covering food packaging, cookware, apparel, juvenile products, and more.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2023: PFAS disclosure on cookware required
Jan 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in cookware
Jan 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in apparel
Jul 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in juvenile products
CA.andersen_note: Large market with multiple PFAS product laws. Check all product lines sold in CA.

CO.name: Colorado
CO.summary: Bans PFAS in food packaging, firefighting foam, ski wax, and personal care products.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2024: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
Jan 1, 2024: Ban on PFAS in ski wax
Jan 1, 2025: Expanded product bans
CO.andersen_note: Products sold in CO subject to packaging and product bans.

VT.name: Vermont
VT.summary: Bans PFAS in food contact materials, cookware, cosmetics, juvenile products, and more.
key_dates: Jul 1, 2023: Ban on PFAS in food contact ware and cookware
Jul 1, 2025: Expanded product category bans
VT.andersen_note: Small market but strong law. Review applicable product categories.

NY.name: New York
NY.summary: Multiple PFAS laws covering food packaging, carpets, cookware, apparel, and children's products.
key_dates: Dec 31, 2022: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
Jan 1, 2025: Ban on PFAS in cookware
Dec 31, 2024: Ban on PFAS in carpets and rugs
NY.andersen_note: Major market with multiple active PFAS laws.

OR.name: Oregon
OR.summary: Broad PFAS framework covering food packaging, cookware, apparel, juvenile products, and more.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2023: PFAS reporting requirements begin
Jan 1, 2025: Phase-out of PFAS in multiple product categories
OR.andersen_note: Review product lines sold in OR against phase-out schedule.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.summary: Comprehensive PFAS restrictions across food packaging, cookware, apparel, and firefighting foam.
key_dates: Dec 31, 2023: Ban on PFAS in food contact substances
Dec 31, 2025: Expanded product bans
CT.andersen_note: Active PFAS law state. Monitor product compliance.

MA.name: Massachusetts
MA.summary: PFAS maximum contaminant levels in drinking water. Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions.
key_dates: Oct 1, 2020: PFAS6 MCL of 20 ppt in drinking water
2022: Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions enacted
MA.andersen_note: Primarily drinking water and firefighting foam focus. Monitor for expanded product legislation.

MI.name: Michigan
MI.summary: Nation's most stringent PFAS drinking water MCLs (7 PFAS compounds). Active remediation program.
key_dates: Aug 2020: PFAS MCLs established for 7 compounds
Ongoing: PFAS remediation and site investigation program
MI.andersen_note: Manufacturing state with strict PFAS water standards. Monitor discharge and facility compliance.

NJ.name: New Jersey
NJ.summary: Very low PFAS MCLs in drinking water. Firefighting foam restrictions. Expanding product laws.
key_dates: 2021: PFOA MCL of 14 ppt, PFOS MCL of 13 ppt established
Ongoing: PFAS site remediation requirements
NJ.andersen_note: Strict water standards. Watch for product legislation expansion.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.summary: Bans PFAS in food packaging and firefighting foam. Limited product scope.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2024: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
2023: Firefighting foam restrictions
MD.andersen_note: Food packaging focus. Monitor for broader product legislation.

RI.name: Rhode Island
RI.summary: PFAS restrictions in food packaging and firefighting foam.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2023: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
2022: Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions
RI.andersen_note: Limited scope currently. Monitor legislative expansion.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.summary: PFAS restrictions on firefighting foam and some reporting requirements.
key_dates: 2022: Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions
Ongoing: PFAS monitoring and reporting programs
IL.andersen_note: Primarily firefighting foam focus. Watch for product expansion.

WI.name: Wisconsin
WI.summary: Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions. Active PFAS monitoring in water.
key_dates: 2022: Ban on PFAS firefighting foam for training
Ongoing: PFAS groundwater monitoring requirements
WI.andersen_note: Key manufacturing state. Firefighting foam restrictions active.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.summary: PFAS MCLs in drinking water. Firefighting foam restrictions.
key_dates: 2020: PFAS MCLs established (12 ppt for PFOA)
2021: Firefighting foam restrictions
NH.andersen_note: Drinking water and firefighting foam focus.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.summary: Firefighting foam PFAS restrictions and some product requirements.
key_dates: Jan 1, 2026: Ban on PFAS in food packaging
2023: Firefighting foam phase-out
HI.andersen_note: Remote market. Monitor food packaging law effective 2026.

DE.name: Delaware
key_dates: Jan 1, 2024: Ban on PFAS in food packaging

MT.name: Montana
key_dates: 2021: PFAS firefighting foam restrictions

AK.name: Alaska
key_dates: 2022: PFAS firefighting foam use restrictions

VA.name: Virginia
VA.summary: Active PFAS legislation proposed covering food packaging and products. PFAS monitoring program active.
key_dates: 2024-2025: Multiple PFAS bills under consideration
VA.andersen_note: Watch for legislation passage. Growing market.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.summary: PFAS contamination hotspot (Cape Fear River). Active proposed legislation.
key_dates: Ongoing: PFAS contamination remediation and proposed product bills
NC.andersen_note: High PFAS contamination awareness. Legislation likely.

PA.name: Pennsylvania
PA.summary: PFAS MCLs for drinking water under development. Product legislation proposed.
key_dates: 2024-2025: PFAS MCL rulemaking in progress
PA.andersen_note: Major manufacturing state. Watch for MCL finalization.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.summary: Limited enacted PFAS law. Some proposed product legislation.
key_dates: 2025: Proposed food packaging restrictions

MO.name: Missouri
MO.summary: Limited PFAS activity. Some proposed firefighting foam restrictions.
key_dates: 2025: Proposed firefighting foam restrictions

NV.name: Nevada
NV.summary: PFAS restrictions proposed for food packaging and firefighting foam.
key_dates: 2025: Proposed PFAS legislation

FL.name: Florida
FL.summary: No significant PFAS-specific state product laws enacted. Limited regulatory activity.
FL.andersen_note: No current PFAS product compliance requirements.

TX.name: Texas
TX.summary: No significant PFAS-specific state laws. Some groundwater monitoring.
TX.andersen_note: No current PFAS product compliance requirements.

WV.name: West Virginia
WV.summary: No significant PFAS state laws enacted. Some PFAS contamination issues.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.summary: Third state to pass comprehensive PFAS law after Maine and Minnesota. HB 212 bans intentionally added PFAS across a broad range of consumer products in three phases. Manufacturer reporting to NMED begins Jan 1, 2027. NMED implementation rules expected July 2026. Notably includes a fluoropolymer exemption (unlike ME/MN).
key_dates: Jul 2026: NMED implementation rules expected
Jan 1, 2027: Phase 1 bans take effect — cookware, food packaging, dental floss, juvenile products, firefighting foam; manufacturer reporting to NMED begins
Jan 1, 2028: Phase 2 bans — carpets, cleaning products, cosmetics, textiles, ski wax, upholstered furniture
Jan 1, 2032: Phase 3 — virtually all non-exempt products banned
NM.andersen_note: EMERGING COMPLIANCE STATE. HB 212 signed Apr 8, 2025 — 3rd comprehensive state PFAS law. Key distinction: fluoropolymer exemption exists (unlike ME/MN), which may benefit Andersen's weatherstripping/seal coatings that use fluoropolymer PFAS. Phase 1 reporting starts Jan 2027 — track NMED rules due Jul 2026. Supply chain risk: NM law creates another registration/reporting obligation for Andersen's component manufacturers selling into NM.
